scope: app_folder_structure plugin workspace and app-workspace contract engine
purpose: Implementation-ready task punches for the Plugin Development Track; no implementation changes are made by this artifact.
global_safety_rules: Do not start implementation until the active Evo slice is approved.
Do not change unrelated plugins or runtime systems.
Do not create app workspaces outside the canonical workspaces/apps/<app_slug>/ root.
Do not overwrite non-empty files without explicit approval or a safe flag.
Do not allow path traversal or unsafe slugs.
Do not invent folder structures outside the approved contract.
Do not auto-migrate compact folders unless the change is explicitly additive.
Do not promote or publish anything directly.

punches.goal: Map repo conventions and safe integration points.
punches.meaning: Learn how this repo already registers plugins, exposes commands, writes runtime state, and documents behavior so the new plugin follows the same rules.
punches.acceptance: A clear analysis report exists with repo conventions and integration points.
tests: Repository inspection only; no behavior changes.

punches.goal: Define the app-folder planning dependency map.
punches.meaning: Convert the phase brief into ordered slices so each later step has a clear start and end.
punches.acceptance: The app-folder plan is split into safe Evo slices.

punches.goal: Create the plugin shell and manifest.
punches.meaning: Establish app_folder_structure as a real plugin package with a manifest and entry point, but no app-workspace logic yet.
punches.acceptance: Plugin exists as a safe shell with documented purpose.
tests: Plugin metadata loads and surfaces a status command.

punches.goal: Add status output and discoverability reporting.
punches.meaning: Give users and workers a read-only way to see what the plugin is for before any workspace logic is added.
punches.acceptance: Status reports plugin state without creating folders.

punches.meaning: List supported workspace types for the app workspace root.
punches.acceptance: Workspace types are listed explicitly.

punches.goal: Validate supported workspace types and reject unsupported ones.
punches.meaning: Fail closed for unrecognized workspace types and unsafe roots.
punches.acceptance: Invalid workspace types are rejected.

punches.goal: Create the fixed app pipeline skeleton.
punches.meaning: Create the canonical app workspace root and the top-level pipeline folders only.
punches.acceptance: A new app workspace has the canonical top-level pipeline.

punches.goal: Write app manifest, state, and hidden metadata scaffolding.
punches.meaning: Add workspace state and manifest files that make the app workspace tractable.
punches.acceptance: App manifest/state exists alongside the pipeline.

punches.meaning: Create the input-only area for uploaded snapshots, files, links, and answers.
punches.acceptance: Viber-provided source material is separated cleanly.

punches.meaning: Prevent generated roadmap/spec material from leaking into the input zone.
punches.acceptance: Inputs and outputs are strictly separated.

punches.meaning: Load approved folder-structure profiles for the selected category/platform.
punches.acceptance: Category profiles are loaded and validated.

punches.meaning: Fail safely when the category profile is missing, invalid, or incomplete.
forbidden_files: Silent fallback to generic roadmap folders
punches.acceptance: Missing profiles stop generation.

punches.meaning: Create only approved UI/UX, system design, and database/storage internals.
punches.acceptance: Roadmaps are reproducible and profile-driven.

punches.meaning: Save a machine-readable record of which profile created which folders.
punches.acceptance: Roadmap evidence is emitted for each creation.

punches.meaning: Make the source area neutral and governed only by later stack/framework selection.
punches.acceptance: Source remains stack-adaptive.

punches.meaning: Record that the real source layout will be created later by the selected stack/framework.
punches.acceptance: The manifest states source tracking only.

punches.meaning: Create product, requirements, and handoff-oriented docs for future teams.
punches.acceptance: Specification placeholders exist.

punches.goal: Add spec index and traceability artifacts.
punches.meaning: Make it easy to see where requirements came from and where they go.
punches.acceptance: Specs are traceable.

punches.meaning: Add branch, commit, issue, PR, and release strategy docs.
forbidden_files: Git commands that mutate unrelated repos
punches.acceptance: Governance folders exist.

punches.goal: Add GitHub and release governance docs.
punches.meaning: Document how version control and releases are governed when GitHub is connected.
punches.acceptance: Release governance is documented.

punches.meaning: Add a place to track large change packages.
punches.acceptance: Evolutions folders exist.

punches.meaning: Add a place to track granular executable punches.
punches.acceptance: Task-punch folders exist.

punches.goal: Add agents, tests, evidence, and reviews folders.
punches.meaning: Add the production governance areas needed for build quality and review.
punches.acceptance: Governance folders exist from agents to reviews.

punches.goal: Add release, owner portal, and archive folders.
punches.meaning: Add release/deployment and owner-facing summary areas plus archive.
punches.acceptance: Release and archive areas exist.

punches.goal: Implement create, validate, repair, print, and manifest commands.
punches.meaning: Add the command surface for managing app workspaces safely.
punches.acceptance: Commands exist and are safe.

punches.goal: Add safe repair, manifest, evidence, and hardening tests.
punches.meaning: Ensure repair is additive only and produces evidence.
forbidden_files: Deletion or overwrite of user content
punches.acceptance: Repair writes evidence and preserves content.

punches.goal: Upgrade existing workspaces additively to the full canonical set.
punches.meaning: Add missing folders/files without deleting compact folders already present.
punches.acceptance: Existing compact workspaces are upgraded additively.

punches.meaning: Show how older compact folders map into the canonical layout.
punches.acceptance: Mapping is documented and preserved.

punches.meaning: Track workspace lifecycle transitions and audit records.
punches.acceptance: Lifecycle and audit records exist.

punches.meaning: Ensure the plugin is documented and the full safety matrix is covered.
punches.acceptance: Final docs and acceptance tests pass.
